answer:
The Gumroad API uses OAuth 2.0 for authentication [1][2][3]. For third-party integrations, you must implement the full OAuth 2.0 flow to obtain an access token [1][3]. For personal use or single-account integrations, you can manually generate an access token directly within your Gumroad account settings under Advanced > Applications [2][3][4].

Once you have an access token, it must be included in every API request using the Authorization: Bearer header [1][5][2].

Example request:
```bash
curl https://api.gumroad.com/v2/user \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer YOUR_ACCESS_TOKEN"
```
